title: Rhaphiolepis stipularis
intro: Rhaphiolepis stipularis, also known as the Chinese hawthorn or hawthorn bush, is a type of flowering shrub native to the mountainous regions of China. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including cardiovascular disease, due to its potential to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Rhaphiolepis stipularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Rhaphiolepis stipularis is often used to make teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to support cardiovascular health and relieve symptoms of indigestion and dyspepsia.
